hypothesis: If Force Delta (U∆) is a valid data-free uncertainty metric for neural network interatomic potentials, then larger U∆ values will correspond to larger true direct-force errors εdirect on both equilibrium and adversarially perturbed (OOD) structures, because U∆ directly measures the mismatch between conservative forces (−∇E) and non-conservative force predictions and should outperform ad-hoc ensemble variance as an uncertainty indicator.
